title: George Municipality concludes Human Rights Month with community march
description: GEORGE NEWS - As part of the closing...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - As part of the closing activities for Human Rights Month, George Municipality - together with civil society organisations and other key stakeholders - joined residents in a human rights march to raise awareness about the significance of Human Rights Day and the values it upholds.


> The march, which took place on Wednesday 26 March, highlighted several pressing issues:


• Access to quality healthcare services

• Addressing systemic corruption

• Improving the response to gender-based violence

• Strengthening accountability for human rights

• Upholding the principles of equality, dignity, and respect for all

Marchers gathered at the Go George hub in Cradock Street between 10:00 and 12:00, where they voiced a collective call for a more just, inclusive, and equitable society.

Reese O’Connor, the human rights violations co-ordinator at Social Health and Empowerment, and Khanyisile Fortuin of the George Municipality’s special programmes - who also serves as regional leader for the LGBTQI sector under the South African National Aids Council - handed over a memorandum to the police (SAPS) and the Department of Health.

Colonel Lionel Kennedy received the memorandum on behalf of SAPS.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
